%% This is the MASTER TEMPLATE. The job agent reads this file and tailors it
%% for each job description via the Claude API.
%%
%% INSTRUCTIONS:
%%   1. Replace all placeholder text below with your actual experience.
%%   2. Compile with: pdflatex base_resume.tex
%%   3. Verify it fits on exactly ONE page.
%%   4. This file is your "config" — update it whenever you gain new experience.
%%      Do NOT edit the per-job PDFs generated by the agent.
%% ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────

\documentclass[10pt, letterpaper]{article}

%% ── Packages ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
\usepackage[
  top=0.5in, bottom=0.5in, left=0.6in, right=0.6in
]{geometry}
\usepackage{enumitem}
\usepackage{hyperref}
\usepackage{fontawesome5}
\usepackage{titlesec}
\usepackage{multicol}
\usepackage{array}
\usepackage{xcolor}
\usepackage[T1]{fontenc}
\usepackage{lmodern}

%% ── Colours ─────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
\definecolor{accent}{RGB}{0, 90, 160}

%% ── Layout tweaks ────────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
\setlength{\parindent}{0pt}
\setlength{\parskip}{0pt}
\pagestyle{empty}

%% ── Section formatting ───────────────────────────────────────────────────────
\titleformat{\section}
  {\color{accent}\bfseries\large}
  {}{0em}{}
  [\color{accent}\titlerule]
\titlespacing{\section}{0pt}{6pt}{3pt}

%% ── List formatting ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
\setlist[itemize]{
  leftmargin=*,
  topsep=1pt,
  itemsep=1pt,
  parsep=0pt,
  label=\textbullet
}

%% ── Custom commands ──────────────────────────────────────────────────────────
\newcommand{\entry}[4]{%
  % #1 = Role title  #2 = Company  #3 = Dates  #4 = Location
  \textbf{#1} \hfill \textit{#3} \\
  \textit{\color{accent}#2} \hfill #4
}

\newcommand{\projectentry}[2]{%
  % #1 = Project name  #2 = tech stack
  \textbf{#1} \hfill \textit{#2}
}

\begin{document}

\section{Summary}
Data Scientist and ML Engineer with 3+ years of experience building and deploying
production machine learning systems. Specialised in LLMs, RAG pipelines, and
recommendation systems. Track record of shipping ML solutions that measurably
impact business metrics.

%%  EXPERIENCE
%% ════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════════

\section{Experience}

\entry{Senior Data Scientist}{Company Name A}{Jan 2024 -- Present}{Remote}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Built a production RAG pipeline using LangChain and Pinecone achieving
        85\% retrieval accuracy at 0.52s average latency, serving 200K daily queries
  \item Designed and shipped a recommendation system (two-tower neural net, PyTorch)
        that increased click-through rate by 18\% on an A/B test over 2M users
  \item Reduced model training time by 60\% by migrating training jobs from EC2 to
        SageMaker with spot instances and distributed training
  \item Mentored 2 junior data scientists and led weekly ML reading group
\end{itemize}

\vspace{4pt}

\entry{Data Scientist}{Company Name B}{Jun 2022 -- Dec 2023}{Boston, MA}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Developed churn prediction model (XGBoost + SHAP) that reduced customer
        churn by 12\%, saving \$2.4M ARR; deployed via FastAPI on AWS Lambda
  \item Built SQL-based ETL pipeline in dbt processing 50GB/day of event data
        into analytics-ready tables, reducing analyst query time by 40\%
  \item Ran 15+ A/B tests end-to-end using internal experimentation platform;
        implemented Bayesian stopping rules to reduce experiment duration by 30\%
  \item Automated weekly reporting with Python + BigQuery, saving 8 hours/week
\end{itemize}

\vspace{4pt}

\entry{Data Science Intern}{Company Name C}{May 2021 -- Aug 2021}{New York, NY}
\begin{itemize}
  \item Trained NLP classifier (BERT fine-tuned) to route 10K+ monthly support
        tickets; achieved 91\% accuracy, reducing manual triage time by 70\%
  \item Scraped and processed 500K product reviews using BeautifulSoup and spaCy
        for sentiment analysis dashboard used by the product team
\end{itemize}
